title: MSI Gaming RTX 5070 Ti 16G Gaming Trio OC Graphics Card (16GB GDDR7, 256-bit, Extreme Performance: 2580, DisplayPort x 3 2.1a, HDMI 2.1b, NVIDIA Blackwell Architecture)
description:
Powered by the NVIDIA Blackwell architecture and DLSS 4
TRI FROZR 4 Thermal Design: Upgraded fans, airflow control, and thermal design deliver superior cooling and quieter operation
STORMFORCE Fan: Seven fan blades, claw texturing, and a circular arc are designed for optimal airflow with minimal noise
Nickel-plated Copper Baseplate: Heat from the GPU and memory is swiftly captured by a nickel-plated copper baseplate and transferred
Core Pipes feature a square design to maximize contact with the GPU baseplate for optimal thermal management

reviews.comment: My PC Specs:CPU: AMD Ryzen 9 9900xMotherboard: MSI 870E Tomahawk WiFiRAM: 32GB G.Skill Ripjaws 6000MHz CL36CPU Cooler: ASUS Prime LC 360PSU: MSI A1000G PCIe 3.0 (1000W)GPU: MSI RTX 5070 Ti Gaming Trio OCCase: Corsair Frame 4000d---I recently upgraded to the MSI RTX 5070 Ti Gaming Trio OC, and I’ve been thoroughly impressed from the moment it arrived. The packaging was premium and secure, with everything neatly organized and well-protected.Installation was straightforward and the card felt solid and well-built. Once up and running, I immediately noticed how quiet it is even under heavy loads, the fans are nearly silent. Thermals are excellent, with all components, including the GPU, running between 50–60°C during intensive use. I also applied a mild undervolt, which helped reduce temperatures slightly while maintaining stable performance.In terms of performance, the card delivers as expected smooth, high-FPS gaming at 1440p and solid creative performance. With my current setup, it’s an excellent match and leaves plenty of headroom for future upgrades.Overall, I’m very satisfied with the MSI RTX 5070 Ti Gaming Trio OC. It's quiet, cool, efficient, and performs exceptionally well out of the box. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a premium GPU experience.
Great gpu for 1440p gaming. Runs quietly, and good overclocker.
Quiet, no coil whine. RGB on it is subtle but very nice looking. Stays relatively cool under load and delivers great performance relative to price point for 1440p gaming.

Years of waiting on upgrading was worth it. Had the goated EVGA1080ti FTW3 but support ends on Oct 2025. Decided to make the leap and swap to MSI and it is performing great. Temps are low and little to no fan noise. A bit longer than the 1080ti I had so needed to get a longer case.

It's a good card. I am using it with a  intel i9 9800k processor and I am able to run ff7 rebirth at the highest settings at 240 fps without really any issues. The price is a bit high but if you can get it on sale and need an upgrade from a much older non rtx card then this is a good choice. My previous card was a gtx 1070 ti so this was leaps and bounds an improvement. It does give off some heat but seems to stay at a reasonable temperature. I am very pleased with it.
Price was steep but works amazing !
Worked as expected, arrived on time .
For the price this is the card to get for sure.
For anyone wondering, description is wrong, it says 5070 and title says 5070ti. Card is 5070ti. Delivered on time Product is perfect as it should be
وصلني الكرت الصحيح ، فيه مراجعات كاتبه تقييم واحد انه كرت غلط ولكن الكرت هو 5070 Ti نفس اللي بالصورةو16gb والنوع صح ومغلف تغليف محكم بس تأخر شحنه مره اكثر من اسبوعين .
وحش بال2k و 4k ما شاء الله تبارك الرحمن
